name: Fennel and Onion Tart
recipeIngredient: 1/2 ounce yeast (fresh) or 1/2 package dry yeast
recipeInstructions:
Fennel-Onion Marmalade:
2 cups water
1 onion, minced
5 garlic cloves, minced
1½ tablespoons fresh thyme
Pearl Onions:
60 pearl onions, peeled
1 cup water
⅓ cup balsamic vinegar
1 teaspoon honey or maple syrup
Assembly:
½ cup chives, snipped
To make the tart-shell dough: In a large bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Add salt and flour; mix well with hands if necessary. Knead until a ball forms. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 3 hours.
To prepare fennel-onion marmalade: Combine water, onion, fennel, garlic, thyme, and honey or syrup in a 3-quart saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 25 minutes, until all the water has evaporated and vegetables are tender. Cool to room temperature.
To prepare pearl onions: In a 2-quart saucepan, combine onions, water, vinegar, and honey. Cook over low heat for 20 minutes, or until water has evaporated and onions are tender. Cool to room temperature.
Assemble the tart: Roll dough into a circle about 8 inches in diameter and place on baking sheet. Spread fennel-onion marmalade over dough, leaving at least half an inch from the edge. Arrange pearl onions on top.
Bake at 350°F for 30-35 minutes, or until shell is golden brown and cooked through. Remove from oven and garnish with chives. Cut into four wedges; serve warm or at room temperature.
